reasoning: For a USDA FSIS recall to be officially marked 'Closed' on their website, the recalling firm must complete product recovery/disposition and submit a closure report, which FSIS must then review and verify through effectiveness checks. This bureaucratic process typically takes many months, commonly exceeding the 3-month window between the issuance date (March 28) and the resolution date (July 1). Additionally, a contamination issue with a cleaning solution is likely a Class I or Class II recall, which demands more stringent effectiveness checks. While Cargill's size and resources might allow them to track and recover the product quickly (especially if distributed primarily to foodservice), the slow administrative turnaround at the federal agency makes a closure within 95 days relatively unlikely. Therefore, I estimate a 20% probability that it will be formally closed on the FSIS website before July 1, 2025.
